El Autor, Oesterland, fue capturado por los "Manos" (basado en la agrupación guerrillera Montoneros) lo que le llevó a un lavado de cabeza causante de que El Eternauta 2 tuviera mucha inclinación política, como si hubiera sido escrita por otra persona. comment_content: Héctor Germán Oesterheld, renowned writer of El Eternauta, was kidnapped in April 1977 by Argentina’s military dictatorship. A member of Montoneros, Oesterheld was detained in La Plata and taken to clandestine detention centers, where he was tortured and eventually murdered, presumably in 1978. His four daughters —Estela, Diana, Marina, and Beatriz— were also kidnapped and disappeared at different times between 1976 and 1977, along with two sons-in-law and several of his grandchildren, some of whom were illegally appropriated. His story became a symbol of state terrorism and the family devastation caused by illegal repression in Argentina.

reply_content: Love The Eternaut, but let’s not forget—it didn’t start the fire. Charles Fort was already talking alien overlords, and by 1947 you had Kenneth Arnold’s flying saucers, and the Maury Island case with UFOs dropping weird material and the first Men in Black showing up. Brazil had wild cases too—João Prestes, Villas Boas—all before the '50s. The Eternaut is awesome, just not ground zero.

It is not bad at all and for when it was written very good indeed. It was also very influential and you can see echoes even to the modern day--'I am legend' most obviously perhaps and even shows like 'The Walking Dead' and 'The Last of Us' owe it a debt. However I wouldn't quite say it marks a before and after point! I'm not sure anything in SF does, unless it is Campbell's Astounding/Analog years.

Nonetheless I am still looking forward to seeing this adaptation.

reply_content:
Ι just hope they don't ruin it as Osterheld did in its final chapters...
But let me explain: I'm Greek and "El Eternauta" used to be published in bimonthly instalments back in the late 80ies, until the magazine that got its rights for Greece got terminated. It was the worst thing that could happen to teenage me, because I ABSOLUTELY LOVED the segment of "El Eternauta". And  ever since, I was randomly thinking about it, till the mid '10s, when thanks to the Internet I came across some PDFs of the whole comic in English. I was ecstatic that I would  finally find out how the story concluded and feel closure to that adolescence trauma of mine,,,but alas, it seems that I had already read the best part of El Eternauta: the rest of the comic I found to be sheer disaster, considering the script.....

reply_content:
@healinggrounds19 It’s a long story, and I’ll do my best to summarize it, as it’s part of a broader issue that still divides Argentine society to this day (these discussions often lead to trolling, but I’ll assume you’re genuinely asking to learn more).

After Juan Perón’s death, his successor and wife, Isabel Perón, faced escalating social conflict and economic crisis. Her weak government became an opportunity for a military junta—backed by right-wing sectors and supported by the CIA’s Operation Condor—to overthrow her in a coup. They imprisoned her and established an authoritarian regime that banned all political opposition.

What followed was a period of state terrorism: covert military and police task forces targeted people based on their political activity (real or suspected). Thousands were kidnapped, tortured, or executed—many buried in unmarked graves, dumped in rivers, or simply "disappeared." Some survivors fled into exile. Among the victims was Héctor Oesterheld, a writer whose four daughters were also kidnapped before he himself vanished. Like many others, his family was labeled "dangerous" simply for their Peronist or left-wing affiliations.

Some still justify these crimes by framing the era as a "Dirty War" (a term implying a two-sided conflict), but this misrepresents reality. There was no war—only a systematic genocide carried out by the state against its own people, denying victims due process or even acknowledgment. Oesterheld and his family are among the 30,000 desaparecidos (disappeared) remembered in Argentina’s ongoing fight for justice.

reply_content: @GatubiDomador Oesterheld was a Peronist and tied to Argentine nationalism, but he wasn’t just that. He joined the Montoneros, a leftist group within Peronism that used armed resistance against the junta. His Che Guevara biography (1968) was banned for its revolutionary stance, and Eternaut II (1976) attacks the dictatorship from a leftist perspective. Being against libertarian ideas was part of it, but his actions and work show he leaned far left. The junta targeted him for that, not just his nationalism.

comment_content: The Eternaut (1957) is a must-read comic for every sci-fi fan. Sadly, its original author was kidnapped and disappeared during Argentina's last dictatorship
reply_content: Héctor Germán Oesterheld, renowned writer of El Eternauta, was kidnapped in April 1977 by Argentina’s military dictatorship. A member of Montoneros, Oesterheld was detained in La Plata and taken to clandestine detention centers, where he was tortured and eventually murdered, presumably in 1978. His four daughters —Estela, Diana, Marina, and Beatriz— were also kidnapped and disappeared at different times between 1976 and 1977, along with two sons-in-law and several of his grandchildren, some of whom were illegally appropriated. His story became a symbol of state terrorism and the family devastation caused by illegal repression in Argentina.

@healinggrounds19 that was the fate of 30.000 victims during our last dictatorship. we call them “desaparecidos” (the disappeared) because when the lead dictator was asked about all these people that’d been kidnapped, he said they were neither dead or alive, they were “desaparecidos”. important to note some of these people were pregnant women, who’s babies were taken from them and given to other families, unable to ever know their true identities or the fate their real parents had faced. the daughters of the author of the eternaut were pregnant when they were kidnapped, and the identities of their babies are still unknown. i invite anyone to learn about our history, it’s a testament to human kind: it’s horrors and its heroes. but be careful: the far right systematically attempts to deny and justify the atrocities committed during this time, so be skeptical about certain narratives.

important to note: you’ll often hear them say the number of desaparecidos was way lower. the number itself is symbolic, because we can’t know for sure: this all happened clandestinely, it’s not like the military was archiving records of their own crimes. the number is an estimate (how they got to that estimate is not random, i invite you to look it all up if this interests you).

comment_content: en argentina juegan truco?
reply_content: @ Si yo tengo dos cartas iguales del mismo palo canto "envido", si tu me dices "quiero", el que gana se lleva 2 puntos y si dices "no quiero" yo me llevo 1. Pero si yo tengo una buena suma, puedo cantar "real envido" que si me dices "quiero", quien gana se lleva 4 y si dices "no quiero", quien canto se lleva 1. Si tengo una cantidad aun mejor (arriba de 30 para alguien que no miente) puedo cantar "falta envido", quien la gana se lleva el partido. Si no es querida, quien la canto, se lleva 1 punto. Es como un sistema de categorías, "envido" es la base, "real envido" intermedio y "falta envido" seria la ultima categoría. Todas se puede cantar desde el inicio, es decir, cantar "falta envido" sin haber pasado por las anteriores, como también se puede ir escalando, ejemplo: alguien canta "envido", en vez de responder "quiero", le respondes "real envido", él en ves de responderte "quiero" te grita "falta envidooooo" y ahí ya se tiene que responder con "quiero" o "no quiero". Espero se haya entendido.

​@vincentprado es un poco mas complejo.

Envido no querido 1, querido 2.
Envido - Envido no querido 2, querido 4
Envido - Real Envido no querido 2, querido 5.
Envido - Envido - Real Envido no querido 4, querido 7.
Envido - Real Envido no querido 2, querido 5
Real Envido no querido 1, querido 3.
Envido - Envido - Real Envido - Falta Envido no querido 7, querido (depende de la regla de Falta Envido)

Falta Envido en las malas (menos de 15 puntos) te llevás el partido, en las buenas te llevas los puntos que le falte a la pareja (o trío) que este mas cerca de los 30.

Es decir que hay combinaciones de los tres cantos, los puntos que se suman en el "No quiero" es la suma de lo cantado anteriormente.

comment_content: en argentina juegan truco?
reply_content: El envido en el truco es una de las apuestas que se hacen durante el juego, relacionada con la suma de puntos de las cartas del mismo palo que tenga un jugador. Es una parte clave del juego que se combina con la estrategia y el engaño. En tal caso el oponente acepta o puede subir la apuesta diciendo Real Envido que vale 5 puntos y le pasa la presión al primero si acepta o no. Este último puede subir aun mas la puesta y decir Falta Envido, en tal caso si se acepta, el que tenga mayor puntaje gana la partida que es a 30 puntos. Todo esto puede ser real que tenga puntos o mentira. Generalmente muchas veces uno que no tiene nada, trata de asustar al contrincante subiendo la apuesta para que este se retire para pasar a la siguiente mano ganando al menos un par de puntos. El truco es un juego de engaños. Y dentro de esto está la "pesca".
